name: Cross River Bank
description_short: Cross River Bank is an FDIC-insured digital banking platform providing API-based financial infrastructure for fintech companies, including payments, lending, and crypto services.
description_long:
Cross River Bank operates as a modern banking infrastructure provider serving fintech companies and financial platforms rather than direct consumers. Founded to support the fintech ecosystem, the bank leverages API-based technology to deliver banking services at scale. The company positions itself as a backend banking partner enabling other financial service providers to launch and scale their own offerings.

Cross River's core services include a full-suite banking platform with FDIC-insured accounts, crypto and stablecoin infrastructure, payment rails (ACH, wires, real-time networks), card issuing and processing, digital lending solutions, and principal financing. They offer specialized solutions for neobanks, social platforms, gaming operators, digital lenders, merchant acquirers, and institutional credit investors. The platform supports unlimited subledgers and multi-rail API-based money movement through a single integration point.

The company distinguishes itself through comprehensive, modular financial infrastructure that allows businesses to mix and match services rather than build from scratch. Recent expansion into AI, crypto, and embedded finance (noted in their $50M funding announcement) reflects evolving market demands. CRB Securities, a Cross River subsidiary, provides capital raising and advisory services including private placements, M&A, ABS issuance, and loan sale advisory specifically for fintech companies.

Cross River operates as a B2B banking service provider, not a direct consumer bank. Consumers do not open accounts directly with Cross River; instead, they access the bank's infrastructure through partner platforms. This is an important distinction—the company's value proposition targets institutional partners and fintech builders, not individual depositors seeking personal banking products. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: FDIC-insured checking and savings accounts with unlimited subledgers
Crypto and stablecoin payment infrastructure with on-chain settlement
ACH, wire transfer, and real-time payment (RTP) rails
Card issuing and processing with Visa and Mastercard networks
Digital lending platform with origination, servicing, securitization, and sale capabilities
Principal financing for cash-flowing assets including credit facilities and loan purchases
CRB Securities investment banking services (private placements, M&A, ABS issuance)
Gaming industry payment and payout solutions
Social platform embedded payments and wallet infrastructure
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by U.S. government full faith and credit
Comprehensive API-based platform integrating accounts, payments, lending, and crypto in single integration
Crypto and stablecoin infrastructure enabling on-chain settlement with bank-grade controls
Serves multiple verticals (gaming, social platforms, neobanks, lenders) with purpose-built solutions
Global payment rail capabilities across ACH, wires, and real-time networks
Demonstrated scale with $32B+ consumer loans facilitated since 2019
CRB Securities provides fintech-specific capital raising and advisory services
cons: B2B-only model—consumers cannot open accounts directly; access only through partner platforms
Pricing and fee structure not disclosed on website; transparency limited for comparison
Requires significant technical integration capability; not suitable for non-technical businesses
Regulatory complexity around crypto and lending services creates ongoing compliance risks
Heavy focus on fintech partnerships means limited support for traditional small businesses
best_for: Fintech startups and neobanks seeking white-label banking infrastructure
Digital lending platforms needing end-to-end loan origination and servicing
Gaming operators requiring secure payment and payout infrastructure
Social platforms and creator economy businesses seeking embedded payments and lending
diagnosis: Cross River Bank is essential for fintech companies, digital lenders, neobanks, and gaming platforms that need enterprise-grade banking infrastructure delivered via API. Critical caveat: this is not a consumer-facing bank—individuals cannot open accounts directly and must access services through a Cross River partner platform.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
